title: sabicea villosa var. sellowii
intro: Sabicea villosa var. sellowii is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, dysentery, and other gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into a tea or tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its health benefits and potential uses.
